req.title: Schedule performance review
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a hard date-gate: scheduled_at must be >= now+7d. Three partitions emerge: (1) valid future dates well beyond 7d (EP positive), (2) dates exactly 7 days from now as the boundary (BVA boundary - both inclusive and exclusive sides), and (3) dates fewer than 7 days away or in the past (NEG/EP negative). A State Transition case covers the workflow from 'draft' to 'scheduled' confirming notification emission. The boundary is split into exact-7d-accepted and exact-6d-rejected to fully exercise BVA. The Decision Table case combines role and date conditions to verify the compound guard.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_future_date
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager (role:manager)
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 14 days (well inside valid partition)
POST to /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a review ID
Both manager and reviewee receive notification (notification flag in response or side-effect assertion)
trace: R-WEB-092: valid date >= now+7d shall result in review scheduled
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import api_client, assert_notification_sent

def test_schedule_review_valid_future_date(<fixture:manager_session>, <fixture:reviewee_id>):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": <fixture:reviewee_id>,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
        "notes": "Annual review"
    }
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        session=<fixture:manager_session>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain a review ID"
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"
    assert_notification_sent(recipient=<fixture:reviewee_id>, review_id=body["id"])
    assert_notification_sent(recipient=<fixture:manager_session>.user_id, review_id=body["id"])

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager (role:manager)
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
Server and client clocks are synchronised to UTC
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + exactly 7 days (BVA lower boundary of valid partition)
expected: HTTP 201 Created (7-day boundary is inclusive per requirement 'at least 7 days')
Response body contains review ID and status = 'scheduled'
trace: R-WEB-092: 'at least 7 days in advance' implies now+7d is the inclusive lower bound
BVA: test exact boundary value on the valid side
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted(
    <fixture:manager_session>, <fixture:reviewee_id>
):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": <fixture:reviewee_id>,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at
    }
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        session=<fixture:manager_session>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Boundary now+7d must be accepted, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_6_days_boundary_rejected
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager (role:manager)
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 6 days 23 hours 59 minutes (one minute below 7-day boundary)
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: Error message indicates the date is too soon / must be at least 7 days in advance
trace: R-WEB-092: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected with 422
BVA: test one unit below the valid boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_schedule_review_6_days_boundary_rejected(
    <fixture:manager_session>, <fixture:reviewee_id>
):
    just_under_7d = timedelta(days=6, hours=23, minutes=59)
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + just_under_7d).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": <fixture:reviewee_id>,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at
    }
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        session=<fixture:manager_session>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 for sub-7d date, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors", body.get("detail", []))
    assert any("scheduled_at" in str(e) for e in errors), (
        "Error payload must reference the scheduled_at field"
    )

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager (role:manager)
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
actions: Set scheduled_at = yesterday (clearly in the past, invalid partition)
trace: R-WEB-092: past dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-092: error pathway 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import api_client, assert_no_notification_sent

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(
    <fixture:manager_session>, <fixture:reviewee_id>
):
    yesterday = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": <fixture:reviewee_id>,
        "scheduled_at": yesterday
    }
    resp = api_client(
        method="POST",
        path="/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        session=<fixture:manager_session>
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Past date must be rejected with 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    assert_no_notification_sent(session=<fixture:manager_session>)

tests.name: test_schedule_review_non_manager_role_rejected
preconditions: User is authenticated with role:employee (not manager)
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
actions: Set scheduled_at = now + 14 days (valid date to isolate role condition)
expected: Decision table: role=employee AND valid_date => denied
trace: R-WEB-092: Actors:manager — only managers may schedule
R-WEB-092: Preconditions role:manager — other roles must be rejected
DT: cross-product of role condition (invalid) x date condition (valid)

tests.name: test_schedule_review_state_transitions_to_scheduled
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager (role:manager)
A reviewee with no active performance review exists
System starts in state: no review / unscheduled
actions: Verify no existing review for reviewee (state: NONE)
POST valid schedule request with scheduled_at = now + 10 days
expected: Notification events exist for both participants
trace: STC: covers full state chain from no review to notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import api_client, assert_notification_sent, get_review_by_id

def test_schedule_review_state_transitions_to_scheduled(
    <fixture:manager_session>, <fixture:fresh_reviewee_id>
):
    # State: NONE — confirm no prior review
    pre = api_client("GET", f"/performance-reviews?reviewee_id={<fixture:fresh_reviewee_id>}",
                     session=<fixture:manager_session>)
    assert pre.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Precondition: reviewee must have no existing review"

    # Transition: NONE -> SCHEDULED
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    resp = api_client("POST", "/performance-reviews/schedule",
                      json={"reviewee_id": <fixture:fresh_reviewee_id>, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
                      session=<fixture:manager_session>)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    review_id = resp.json()["id"]

    # Verify SCHEDULED state
    review = get_review_by_id(review_id, session=<fixture:manager_session>)
    assert review["status"] == "scheduled"

    # Verify NOTIFIED state
    assert_notification_sent(recipient=<fixture:fresh_reviewee_id>, review_id=review_id)
    assert_notification_sent(recipient=<fixture:manager_session>.user_id, review_id=review_id)
